title: Authors and Citation
text: Stamm (2026). riot: R Inputs/Outputs Tractography. R package version 2.0.0, https://github.com/tractoverse/riot.
code: @Manual{,   title = {riot: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography},   author = {Aymeric Stamm},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 2.0.0},   url = {https://github.com/tractoverse/riot}, }

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: riot (R Input/Output Tractography) package provides R interface importing exporting tractography data R. Currently supported importing formats : native VTK .vtk .vtp files; medInria .fds files; MRtrix .tck/.tsf files; , TrackVis .trk files. package reads tractography data S7 objects defined fiber package: streamline — single fibre tract stored three typed slots: @points (n×3n \times 3 matrix columns X, Y, Z), @point_data (named list per-point numeric vectors, e.g. diffusion metrics sampled every point), @streamline_data (named list per-streamline scalar attributes). bundle — ordered collection streamline objects @streamlines, plus optional bundle-level metadata @bundle_data. read_bundle() returns streamline file contains single tract bundle otherwise. optional bundle_data argument lets attach named list bundle-level metadata (e.g. subject ID, hemisphere, affine transform) directly import time: list stored @bundle_data slot returned bundle. package also allows write bundles back following exporting formats: native VTK .vtk .vtp files; , medInria .fds files.

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: can install released version riot CRAN : Alternatively can install development version riot GitHub :

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
code: uf_left_vtk <- read_bundle(system.file(   "extdata",   "UF_left.vtk",   package = "riot" )) uf_left_vtk #> <bundle [2042 streamlines | 9–33 pts/streamline]> uf_left_vtp <- read_bundle(system.file(   "extdata",   "UF_left.vtp",   package = "riot" )) uf_left_vtp #> <bundle [2042 streamlines | 9–33 pts/streamline]>

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
code: uf_left_fds <- read_bundle(system.file(   "extdata",   "UF_left.fds",   package = "riot" )) uf_left_fds #> <bundle [2042 streamlines | 9–33 pts/streamline]>

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
code: af_left_tck <- read_bundle(system.file(   "extdata",   "AF_left.tck",   package = "riot" )) af_left_tck #> <bundle [5000 streamlines | 8–54 pts/streamline]>

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
code: cc_mid_trk <- read_bundle(system.file(   "extdata",   "CCMid.trk",   package = "riot" )) cc_mid_trk #> <bundle [525 streamlines | 29–270 pts/streamline]>

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
code: uf_left_meta <- read_bundle(   system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtk", package = "riot"),   bundle_data = list(subject = "sub-01", hemisphere = "left") ) uf_left_meta@bundle_data #> $subject #> [1] "sub-01" #>  #> $hemisphere #> [1] "left"

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: riot relies fiber package S7 classes streamline bundle represent tractography data R, well constructors, predicates, geometry methods operate . riot depend S7 directly.

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: Since version 1.2.0, riot delegates VTK linkage rvtk infrastructure package. VTK system requirement: neither riot rvtk. install time, rvtk searches pre-installed VTK following order: VTK_DIR environment variable (highest priority). Homebrew — macOS (brew install vtk). pkg-config — macOS Linux. Well-known system prefix paths (/usr, /usr/local) — Linux. Rtools42+ pacman package active MSYS2 environment (e.g. mingw-w64-ucrt-x86_64-vtk UCRT64) — Windows. pre-installed VTK found, rvtk automatically downloads pre-compiled static shared VTK build GitHub releases, manual VTK installation ever required.

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: riot bundles TinyXML-2. tinyxml2.cpp modified avoid use stdout printf per Writing R Extensions manual recommendations R input/output mechanism writing console.

title: R Inputs/Outputs for Tractography
text: authors like thank Tim Schäfer, author freesurferformats package, helpful code read MRtrix TrackVis tractography file formats.

what: Convert a bundle (or streamline) to a flat named list for the C++ writers — bundle_to_flat_list
title: Convert a bundle (or streamline) to a flat named list for the C++ writers — bundle_to_flat_list
text: Reconstructs X, Y, Z, PointId, StreamlineId columns (plus extra per-point attribute columns per-streamline attributes broadcast points) expected WriteVTK(), WriteVTP(), WriteFDS().

title: Convert a bundle (or streamline) to a flat named list for the C++ writers — bundle_to_flat_list

text: x bundle streamline.

what: Convert a flat named list (C++ output) to a streamline or bundle — flat_list_to_bundle
title: Convert a flat named list (C++ output) to a streamline or bundle — flat_list_to_bundle
text: input named list returned ReadVTK(), ReadVTP(), ReadFDS(), minimum columns "X", "Y", "Z", "PointId", "StreamlineId". Columns named streamline_cols treated per-streamline attributes: one value stored per streamline (taken first occurrence group rather broadcast). remaining extra columns treated per-point attributes.

title: Convert a flat named list (C++ output) to a streamline or bundle — flat_list_to_bundle
Convert a flat named list (C++ output) to a streamline or bundle — flat_list_to_bundle
text: lst named list least columns "X", "Y", "Z", "PointId", "StreamlineId". streamline_cols Character vector column names store per-streamline data.  Defaults character(0).

title: Convert a flat named list (C++ output) to a streamline or bundle — flat_list_to_bundle
text: streamline bundle.

title: Convert a flat named list (C++ output) to a streamline or bundle — flat_list_to_bundle
text: Returns streamline data contain exactly one streamline, otherwise bundle.

title: Import bundles into R — read_bundle
text: go-function import bundles R. Based VTK DIPY, currently support eight different formats detailed documentation input argument file.

title: Import bundles into R — read_bundle
text: file string specifying path file containing tractography data. Currently supported files : standard VTK formats .vtk .vtp, medInria .fds format, MRtrix .tck/.tsf format, TrackVis .trk .trx formats, DIPY .dpy format, .fib format. reference_file string specifying path reference image file. required importing .trx, .fib, .dpy files, formats contain spatial information image space. reference image used correctly position bundle appropriate space. Default NULL. bundle_data named list bundle-level metadata store @bundle_data slot returned bundle object (e.g. affine transform used tracking, subject identifier, scalar/vector annotation). Ignored warning file contains single streamline (case streamline returned). Default list().

title: Import bundles into R — read_bundle
text: bundle object file contains multiple streamlines, streamline object contains exactly one. streamline numeric matrix least three named columns "X", "Y", "Z" (one row per point along tract). Additional per-point scalar attributes, present source file, appear extra named columns. bundle returned bundle_data non-empty, @bundle_data slot populated supplied list.

title: Import bundles into R — read_bundle
code: uf_left_vtk <- read_bundle(system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtk",  package = "riot")) #> Number of data points: 38697 #> Number of streamlines: 2042 #> ✔ The bundle stored in /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/riot/extdata/UF_left.vtk has been successfully imported.  # Attach bundle-level metadata uf_left_vtk2 <- read_bundle(   system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtk", package = "riot"),   bundle_data = list(subject = "sub-01", hemisphere = "left") ) #> Number of data points: 38697 #> Number of streamlines: 2042 #> ✔ The bundle stored in /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/riot/extdata/UF_left.vtk has been successfully imported. uf_left_vtk2@bundle_data #> $subject #> [1] "sub-01" #>  #> $hemisphere #> [1] "left" #>

title: Export bundles from R — write_bundle
text: function exports bundle stored bundle object file one supported formats. Supported formats include VTK (.vtk), VTP (.vtp), FDS (.fds), TRK (.trk), TCK (.tck), TRX (.trx), FIB (.fib), DPY (.dpy). formats require reference image (TRX, FIB, DPY), user must provide path reference image file.

title: Export bundles from R — write_bundle
text: x bundle object. file string specifying path file containing tractography data. Currently supported files : standard VTK formats .vtk .vtp, medInria .fds format, MRtrix .tck/.tsf format, TrackVis .trk .trx formats, DIPY .dpy format, .fib format. reference_file string specifying path reference image file. required importing .trx, .fib, .dpy files, formats contain spatial information image space. reference image used correctly position bundle appropriate space. Default NULL.

title: Export bundles from R — write_bundle
text: Warning: rely DIPY provide support save bundles .trk, .trx, .tck, .dpy .fib formats. Among formats, .trk .trx formats able keep track additional attributes assigned either streamlines points.

title: Export bundles from R — write_bundle
code: uf_left  <- read_bundle(system.file("extdata", "UF_left.vtp",  package = "riot")) #> Number of data points: 38697 #> Number of streamlines: 2042 #> v The bundle stored in /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/riot/extdata/UF_left.vtp has been successfully imported. if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ out <- fs::file_temp(ext = ".vtp") write_bundle(uf_left, file = out) } # }

title: riot (development version)
text: read_bundle() gains bundle_data argument. optional named list can now passed read_bundle() stored @bundle_data slot returned bundle object. makes easy attach bundle-level metadata (e.g. subject ID, hemisphere, affine transform) import time without separate assignment step. file contains single streamline argument ignored warning.

title: riot (development version)
text: C++ source files renamed: fascicle* → bundle*. src/fascicleReaders.{cpp,h} src/fascicleWriters.{cpp,h} renamed src/bundleReaders.{cpp,h} src/bundleWriters.{cpp,h} align bundle terminology used throughout package API. include guards updated accordingly (_BUNDLEREADERS_H, _BUNDLEWRITERS_H). functional change.

title: riot (development version)
text: TRK reader now dramatically faster (~20× real-world data). Two bottlenecks removed: /O layer: old read_trk() called readBin() twice per streamline (num_points, point block), causing 2 × N sequential file reads. new C++ function ReadTRK() replaces R loop: reads entire file body single fread() call, makes two fast -memory passes — one count total points pre-allocate output vectors, one parse coordinates scalars using direct memcpy-based reads optional byte-swapping. Bundle assembly: flat_list_to_bundle() previously evaluated sid == inside loop streamlines, making conversion O(streamlines × points). now calls split(seq_along(sid), sid) (C-level operation, O(points)) compute per-streamline index vectors front. fix also benefits VTK/VTP/FDS readers, share conversion function.

title: riot (development version)
text: Windows R CMD check fix: quarto CLI TMPDIR error. R-CMD-check workflow now sets QUARTO_PATH=nonexistent Windows running check. quarto R package calls system2("quarto", "-V", env = paste0("TMPDIR=", …)), Windows passes env assignment positional argument CLI rather setting environment variable, causing ERROR: Unknown command "TMPDIR=…". Setting QUARTO_PATH nonexistent value makes find_quarto() return NULL immediately, system2 never called. Windows R CMD check fix: VTK symbols undefined / linker failures. configure, configure.win, cleanup, cleanup.win, src/Makevars.removed. replaced static src/Makevars uses POSIX backtick syntax (per Writing R Extensions) call tools/configure.R directly compile time: tools/configure.R calls rvtk::CppFlags() rvtk::LdFlagsFile(path = "vtk_libs.rsp") (modules argument). LdFlagsFile() writes complete list static VTK libraries src/vtk_libs.rsp returns @vtk_libs.rsp, linker reads directly — avoiding Windows command-line length limits. modules argument dropped rvtk:::filter_libs() matches module names directly .filenames: Windows files named libvtkIOLegacy-9.5.module names "VTK_IOLegacy" (uppercase prefix + underscore), none matched .rsp file contained VTK libraries. macOS/Linux system VTK (e.g. homebrew), filter_libs called, modules -op anyway. macOS R CMD check fix: _NSEventTrackingRunLoopMode found. tools/configure.R calls rvtk::LdFlagsFile() explicit modules argument, linking VTK /O Common modules riot actually uses. Previously, rvtk fell back pre-built static bundle (case CRAN binary macOS CI), -Wl,-all_load forced every symbol every .— including rendering modules reference _NSEventTrackingRunLoopMode AppKit.framework — riot., causing dlopen() fail check time.

title: riot 2.0.0
text: Breaking change: read_tractogram() write_tractogram() renamed read_bundle() write_bundle() consistency bundle data model.

title: riot 2.0.0
text: optional DIPY Python dependency now declared via reticulate::py_require("dipy") (reticulate ≥ 1.41) instead previous manual py_module_available() check. Reticulate automatically provision DIPY ephemeral virtual environment one DIPY-backed formats (.trx, .fib, .dpy) first used.

what: S7 data model: streamline and bundle classes moved to {fiber}
title: riot 2.0.0
text: @points: n×3n \times 3 numeric matrix columns "X", "Y", "Z" ordered coordinates nn points along tract. PointId implicit row order longer stored. @point_data: named list numeric vectors length nn, holding per-point scalar attributes (e.g. fractional anisotropy point). @streamline_data: named list numeric scalars (length-1 vectors) holding per-streamline attributes (e.g. tract-level weight mean FA). @streamlines: list streamline objects. StreamlineId implicit list position longer stored. @bundle_data: named list bundle-level metadata (e.g. affine transform used tracking). riot longer depends S7 directly; S7 classes, constructors, predicates, methods (streamline(), is_streamline(), bundle(), is_bundle(), format(), print(), length(), [[, [, …) provided fiber re-exported . riot now imports fiber instead S7. read_bundle() returns streamline file contains exactly one tract, bundle otherwise. write_bundle() accepts streamline bundle objects. readr package longer dependency.

what: C++ layer: elimination of intermediate CSV files
title: riot 2.0.0
text: Breaking change (internal): C++ reader functions (ReadVTK, ReadVTP, ReadFDS) longer write temporary CSV file. now return cpp11::writable::list directly R, eliminating temporary file /O associated string-reference issues. C++ writer functions (WriteVTK, WriteVTP, WriteFDS) longer read temporary CSV. now accept cpp11::list directly R. helper WriteCSV (vtkPolyData → CSV) ReadCSV (CSV → vtkPolyData) replaced PolyDataToList ListToPolyData respectively, operating entirely memory.

title: riot 2.0.0
text: VTK discovery, compilation flags, pre-built static library distribution now handled entirely rvtk package (pending CRAN submission). rvtk supplies pre-compiled VTK headers libraries manual VTK installation required platforms: honours user-supplied VTK_DIR environment variable first, tries Homebrew (macOS), pkg-config (macOS/Linux), well-known system prefixes; Windows checks VTK_DIR, Rtools45 pacman, common MSYS2 prefixes; suitable system VTK found downloads pre-built static libraries automatically rvtk GitHub releases. rvtk listed Imports dependency; downstream packages longer need locate VTK . Remotes: astamm/rvtk field removed DESCRIPTION rvtk available CRAN.

title: riot 1.2.0
text: Breaking change system requirements: riot longer bundles VTK source files. Instead, links externally installed VTK (>= 9.1.0) compile time. VTK must present host installing package. shared static VTK builds supported; static builds macOS Linux must compiled -fPIC. user-supplied VTK_DIR environment variable. Homebrew (macOS). pkg-config (macOS Linux). Well-known system include paths (/usr, /usr/local) (Linux). Rtools42+ pacman package active MSYS2 environment (e.g. mingw-w64-ucrt-x86_64-vtk UCRT64) (Windows). Windows, VTK loaded dynamically run time via addDLLDirectory() avoid bundle VTK runtime DLLs inside package. Removed bundled VTK source files, reducing installed package size considerably. Updated SystemRequirements DESCRIPTION document external VTK dependency.

title: riot 1.1.1
text: Modified VTK source files avoid compilation warnings arising using LLVM Apple clang GNU gcc compilers. Now ships shrunk version VTK source files avoid unsuccessful downloads VTK website. Fix compilation errors vtkzlib raised clang16.

title: riot 1.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2023-01-06 Update VTK v9.2.4; Avoid prototype checks vtkzlib using LLVM Clang compiler.

title: riot 1.0.0
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-10 first major release, : Added support read MRtrix .tck/.tsf file formats (#5). Added support read TrackVis .trk file formats (#5). Use one core compile VTK compliance CRAN policy (thanks Prof. B. Ripley). Added tilde expansion file paths. make first major release consider popular tractography formats now supported riot. chose design support VTK medInria file formats writing.

title: riot 0.0.1
text: CRAN release: 2022-05-05 Added NEWS.md file track changes package. Supports reading writing .vtk, .vtp .fds file formats.
